learning_outcomes:
RELS 1.  |  Major
programme:
Knowledge: Graduates will
have acquired the following:
(1)  |  The
understanding of religion in its historical settings and contemporary
contexts of modern living, globally and locally.
(2)  |  The
understanding of religion in its different dimensions.
(3)  |  The
understanding of religion through a crtical analysis.
(4)  |  A
basic knowledge of major religious traditions in the world.
Skills: Graduates will
have acquired the following:
(1)  |  Communication
and presentational (oral and written) skills.
(2)  |  Analytical
and problem solving skills.
(3)  |  Social
skills and team-work skills.
(4)  |  Skills
of critical intelligence and appreciative thinking.
(5)  |  Skills
in cross-cultural understanding and intercultural literacy.
(6)  |  Research
skills.
Attitude: Graduates will
have acquired the following:
(1)  |  Awareness
of the plurality and multi-faceted complexity within religious traditions of
the issues of multiple and conflicting interpretations of religious
traditions.
(2)  |  Self-critical
awareness of pre-suppositions and recognition.
(3)  |  A
critical and yet sympathetic understanding and appreciation of religious
traditions especially those to which one does not belong.
(4)  |  Sensitivity
to the ultimate concern and the religious dimension of human life.
2.  |  Minor
programme:
1. Cultural Studies Upon
the completion of the Minor programme, students will have demonstrated basic
understanding of fundamental issues related to the subject, acquired
knowledge of some aspects of the subject with sufficient exposure and at a
suitable level to allow further study in this subject or related subjects or
to pursue a related career
2. Film and Screen
Studies
Upon completion of the
programme, students will have acquired the following learning outcomes in
three aspects: I. Knowledge outcomes: a. Demonstrate a critical appreciation of some of the dominant
concepts, methods, and debates informing the study of film and screen. b. Demonstrate a capacity to analyze the form and content of
particular audio-visual products. c. Demonstrate an awareness of the variety of ways in which audio-visual products and aspects of individual and collective
social lives are mutually constituted. d. Demonstrate an understanding of different traditions of film and screen practices in different national
and international contexts. II. Skills outcomes: a. Demonstrate skills in the close formal and structural analysis
of different kinds of audio- visual products. b. Demonstrate skills in the research and evaluation of relevant
critical and historical materials
for the study of film and screen. c. Demonstrate an ability to interrelate texts and discourses
specific to their own discipline with issues in the wider context of cultural
and intellectual history. d. Demonstrate an ability to understand and analyze relevant
theoretical ideas and to apply these ideas to audio-visual forms. III. Attitude outcomes: a. Through seminar work and group presentations, demonstrate
communication skills and an ability to work both individually and in groups. b. Acknowledge the contested and provisional nature of knowledge
and understanding and show openness
towards diverse positions and respect for differing views.

Students are required to complete a
minimum of 69 units of courses as follows:

CURE
1009, 1010, 2001[1001]*, 2006[1006]*, 3007, 4008[3008]

(a)  |  Any 2

CURE courses at 2000 or above level, with at least 6 courses from
